generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OperationalTraceLogger : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects operational process traces from the physical system for contextual data aggregation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalTraceDataTransmitted
]

instance IoTSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Network of IoT sensors collecting real-time telemetry data from physical objects"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IoTSensorDataTransmitted
]

instance PhysicalSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic sensor capturing physical system parameters (e.g., temperature, pressure, vibration)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PhysicalSystemDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OperationalTraceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalTraceLogger
    base:desc "One-way data flow of operational traces from physical processes to digital representation"
]

instance IoTSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IoTSensorNetwork
    base:desc "One-way data flow of IoT telemetry from physical objects to digital twin"
]

instance PhysicalSystemDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalSystemSensor
    base:desc "One-way data flow of sensor readings from physical system components"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataTraceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured digital representation of aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance HeterogeneousDataIntegrationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model integrating disparate data sources (sensor, operational, simulation) into cohesive digital representation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance OperationalTraceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed operational traces from physical system processes"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalTraceDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance IoTSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed IoT telemetry data from physical objects"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IoTSensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance PhysicalSystemData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed sensor readings from physical system components"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalSystemDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance SimulationOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data generated from simulation environments (e.g., MathWorks Simulink)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component performing collection, aggregation, and abstraction of contextual data traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataInterpretationService, ContextualDataAggregationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component reconciling heterogeneous data sources using ADLs and modeling frameworks"
    DTDFVocab:enables HeterogeneousDataIntegrationService
]

instance SimulationIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration layer for simulation outputs (e.g., Simulink) with digital twin data"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling environment for multi-layered system descriptions"
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalModelingService, LogicalArchitectureService
]

instance MontiArcADLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc ADL environment for component-connector architecture definitions"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentConnectorModelingService
]

instance MathWorksSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for detailed behavioral modeling and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataInterpretationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active component analyzing one-way data flows to derive actionable insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataInterpretationInsight
]

instance ContextualDataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service collecting, processing, and structuring data traces for specific purposes"
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
]

instance HeterogeneousDataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating multi-perspective stakeholder views through unified modeling"
    DTDFVocab:provides IntegratedDataInsight
]

instance FunctionalModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting multi-layered system descriptions using SysML IBDs"
    DTDFVocab:provides FunctionalArchitectureInsight
]

instance LogicalArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service defining logical architecture components and interfaces"
    DTDFVocab:provides LogicalArchitectureInsight
]

instance ComponentConnectorModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service defining component-connector architectures using MontiArc ADL"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentConnectorInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling state machine and simulation-based behavioral modeling"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationBehaviorInsight
]

instance AutonomousDecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling predictive, optimization, or control services without external software"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataInterpretationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Actionable insights derived from analyzed physical-to-virtual data flows"
]

instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured and purpose-driven contextual data traces for specific applications"
]

instance IntegratedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified digital representation integrating heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives"
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multi-layered functional architecture descriptions using SysML IBDs"
]

instance LogicalArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Logical architecture components and interface definitions"
]

instance ComponentConnectorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Component-connector architecture definitions using MontiArc ADL"
]

instance SimulationBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral simulation results from state machines and dynamic system models"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for predictive maintenance or process optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Centralized environment containing all digital twin components and services"
    base:contains ContextualDataTraceModel, HeterogeneousDataIntegrationModel,
                   DataAggregationEnabler, DataIntegrationEnabler, SimulationIntegrationEnabler,
                   SysMLModelingEnabler, MontiArcADLEnabler, MathWorksSimulinkEnabler,
                   DataInterpretationService, ContextualDataAggregationService,
                   HeterogeneousDataIntegrationService, FunctionalModelingService,
                   LogicalArchitectureService, ComponentConnectorModelingService,
                   BehavioralSimulationService, AutonomousDecisionSupportService
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in this context is implicitly tied to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which rely on **multi-notation modeling tools** (e.g., SysML for architecture, ADLs like ACME/AADL/MontiArc for component-connector views, and simulation tools like MathWorks Simulink or general-purpose languages like C++/Java for behavior). The orchestration and coordination of the DT system would require **architecture patterns** such as **component-based integration**, where components (e.g., digital shadows, simulation models, or active processing units) are interconnected via **interfaces, connectors, and composition rules** defined in ADLs. Resource management would involve **heterogeneous toolchains** (e.g., combining SysML IBDs with Simulink for dynamic behavior), while system-wide coordination would depend on **stakeholder-specific perspectives** and **model synchronization** across layers (e.g., aligning logical architecture with physical implementations). The document does not specify explicit orchestration frameworks (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) but implies a **hybrid, model-driven approach** to DT coordination."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** through **validation and verification (V&V) methods** aligned with MBSE practices. Model fidelity is ensured by **multi-layered abstraction** (e.g., functional/logical/physical architectures) and **behavioral modeling** (e.g., state machines or MontiArc Automaton). Uncertainty sources are addressed via **data aggregation and abstraction** (as defined in the Digital Shadow Reference Model) and **error quantification** through **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) or **analytical models**. Quality assurance processes involve **cross-tool validation** (e.g., SysML for structural integrity, simulation tools for dynamic behavior) and **stakeholder-driven integration** to reconcile heterogeneous perspectives. While specific **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quantitative uncertainty metrics** are not detailed, the framework implies reliance on **automated data processing** (e.g., passive Digital Shadow data flows) and **active DT components** for real-time adjustments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is implicitly tied to the **heterogeneous modeling and simulation tools** referenced in the document. While no explicit hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise) are specified, the integration of **modeling languages (SysML, ADLs like AADL/ACME/MontiArc)**, **simulation tools (Simulink, other simulation methods [29]–[30])**, and **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)** suggests a distributed deployment model. **Computational and storage requirements** are likely high due to the need to handle complex models across multiple abstraction layers (requirements, functional/logical architecture, physics). **Scalability mechanisms** are not detailed, but the use of diverse tools implies modularity and potential for scaling via middleware or orchestration frameworks. **Deployment models** may include hybrid approaches (e.g., edge for real-time data processing and cloud for heavy computation), though specifics are absent. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not addressed, but the reliance on active components (e.g., for DT functionality) implies redundancy or fault-tolerant designs may be required."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on **horizontal integration** with external systems, such as data exchange protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST/HTTP) or interoperability standards (e.g., ISO 22400 for DTs, GAIA-X for data sovereignty). However, the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) suggests integration with **physical objects** via **one-way automated data flows**, implying dependencies on **sensors/actuators** and potentially **enterprise systems** (e.g., ERP, MES) for contextual data aggregation. The MBSE context further implies integration with **external modeling tools** (e.g., Simulink, C++/Java environments) and **simulation platforms** for behavior validation. While no specific integration patterns (e.g., API gateways, message brokers) or DT-to-DT coordination mechanisms are described, the **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** requirement hints at a need for **standardized interfaces** and **semantic interoperability** to bridge disparate systems."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system, as described in the provided documents, integrates **cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **RAMI4.0 (Reference Architecture Model Industrie 4.0)** standards ([48], [49]), which emphasize hierarchical access control and role-based authentication mechanisms within the **Automation Pyramid** ([42]). Specifically, **Digital Shadows** (a concept introduced in [42]) are employed at multiple levels of the pyramid to ensure **fail-safe mechanisms** and **threat mitigation strategies**, including real-time monitoring of asset states and operational data. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** ([46], [47], [50]) serves as a standardized framework for encapsulating asset metadata, operational data, and security policies, enabling **authentication systems** via **OAuth 2.0** and **JWT (JSON Web Tokens)** for secure communication ([50]). Additionally, the **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]) supports **tool-independent exchange of simulation models**, ensuring interoperability while maintaining **risk assessment approaches** through validated model-driven engineering methodologies ([43]). The system leverages **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** via **Mosquitto** ([52]) for lightweight, secure IoT communication, and **Thingsboard (v4.0.0)** ([53]) provides **access control mechanisms** (e.g., RBAC) and **data encryption** for IoT device integration. Safety-critical considerations are addressed through **structured learning of robot dynamics** ([45]), including **friction modeling** for predictive maintenance and failure prevention in robotic systems (e.g., **MABI robotic arms** ([44]))."
]
